# Notes

Loose working notes for the project. Active backlog lives in [docs/TODO.md](../docs/TODO.md); long-form spec lives in [docs/Hallucination_Inc_PRD.md](../docs/Hallucination_Inc_PRD.md).

## Open questions

- Is 30 days the right run length, or should it flex to 45/60 with proportional debt?
- Do we want a deterministic seed mode for sharing "same-game" runs between players?
- Should the simulator ship a baseline policy report so balance changes have a reference number to move against?

## Things to watch for in PRs

- Any new action that spends cash or consumes inventory needs to be reflected in the bankruptcy oracle.
- Any new event needs to round-trip through the daily-tick sequence (interest → build → decay → event → drift → rotation).
- Tone of new UI copy — keep the meme-y register, don't drift into corporate.

## Parking lot

- "Sandbox mode" with debt off and infinite days, for poking at the economy.
- Per-provider event affinities (Anthropic-flavored events vs. Meta-flavored).
- A daily news ticker that surfaces multiple small events instead of one headline.

## Changelog

- **2026-05-28** — **Repo restructure: `src/` for program modules, `tests/` for tooling, `docs/` for backlog.** Moved `engine.py`, `terminal.py`, `web.py`, `templates/`, `static/` into `src/`; moved `run_tests.py` and `simulate.py` into `tests/`; moved `TODO.md` into `docs/`. Only the launcher (`hallucination_inc.py`) stays in the root, plus `README.md`/`CLAUDE.md` (CLAUDE stays so Claude Code auto-loads it). Cross-module imports stayed absolute (`import engine`); `src/` is put on `sys.path` by the launcher, and by a new `tests/_bootstrap.py` that every test + `simulate.py` imports first (before `import engine`) so both the coverage runner and `python3 -m unittest discover -s tests` work. `tests/run_tests.py` recomputes `PROJECT_ROOT`/`SRC_DIR` from its own location and gates coverage on `src/`. Dockerfile gunicorn gained `--pythonpath src`; `web.py`'s `Flask(__name__)` now resolves `src/templates` + `src/static` (verified via test client: `/` 200, CSS 200). Pre-commit hook now runs `python3 tests/run_tests.py`. Docs (CLAUDE.md, README.md, architecture.md, ADR 002/004 links) updated. All 190 tests pass; coverage unchanged.
- **2026-05-28** — **Moved deployment config into `fly/`.** Relocated `fly.toml`, `Dockerfile`, and `.dockerignore` from the repo root into `fly/` (via `git mv`). The Docker build context stays at the repo root so the Dockerfile's `COPY . .` still reaches the app source — the GitHub Actions workflow now deploys with `flyctl deploy --remote-only --config fly/fly.toml --dockerfile fly/Dockerfile --ignorefile fly/.dockerignore .`. Refreshed `.dockerignore` for the new layout (`tests/` and `scripts/` replace the old root-level `test_*.py`; added `fly/`). `flyctl config validate -c fly/fly.toml` passes; couldn't test the actual image build (no local Docker). Docs updated: CLAUDE.md + README.md trees, architecture.md, ADR 004.
- **2026-05-28** — **Moved tests into `tests/`.** Relocated `test_engine.py`, `test_terminal.py`, `test_web.py`, and `test_helpers.py` from the project root into a `tests/` directory (via `git mv`). `run_tests.py` discovery now points at `tests/` and explicitly inserts `PROJECT_ROOT` on `sys.path` so the suites still `import engine`/`terminal`/`web` (project root) alongside `test_helpers` (tests dir) regardless of cwd — no `__init__.py` needed. Pre-commit hook trigger pattern simplified to `'\.py$|^tests/'` and the installed `.git/hooks/pre-commit` re-synced. Docs (CLAUDE.md, README.md) tree blocks updated. All 190 tests pass; coverage unchanged (93.3% engine / 96.0% terminal / 100% web).
- **2026-05-28** — **Web frontend smoke tests + coverage gate.** Added `test_web.py` — 36 Flask-test-client tests covering the welcome → game transition, every action route's happy path + validation branches, `/new` reset, the stale-session bailout (regression test for cbf290c), every `_*` view helper, and both end-screen renderers. `web.py` added to `run_tests.py`'s `TARGET_FILES`; it clears the 90% gate at 100% (208 stmts, 16 stmt-lines not hit — mostly the `main()` entry point and a couple of defensive branches). Fixed a long-standing display bug in `run_tests.py` where tracer hits on non-stmt lines (Flask decorators, multi-line expressions) inflated cover past 100%; capped at 100.0% for display, calc semantics unchanged. Total tests: 154 → 190.
- **2026-05-28** — **Fly.io deploy config added.** Wrote `Dockerfile` (python:3.12-slim, gunicorn `-w 1` so the module-level `_games` dict stays consistent), `.dockerignore` (excludes tests, sim, docs, `__pycache__`, `.git`), and `fly.toml` (app `hallucination-inc`, region `fra`, `auto_stop_machines = "off"`, `min_machines_running = 1`, `shared-cpu-1x` / 256MB). Added `gunicorn` to `requirements.txt`. ADR 004 captures the rationale: in-memory state means single instance / single worker / always-on; alternatives (Cloud Run, Render, DO VM, Vercel) rejected because they'd either wipe state on scale events or add ops surface area without payoff at this stage. No CI/CD pipeline yet — deferred until a second contributor joins or we want auto-deploy on `main` push (sketch in the ADR). First `fly deploy` is the next step and stays a manual operation on my account. TODO updated: checked off the hosting decision, added follow-ups for the first deploy, CI later, and externalizing `_games` when we want scale-to-zero or multi-instance.
- **2026-05-27** — **Engine / frontend split, step 4 (web frontend).** Added `web.py` — a small Flask app that imports `engine` directly and maps HTTP requests onto engine actions. Routes: GET `/` renders the state, POST `/buy`, `/craft`, `/sell`, `/travel`, `/next`, `/borrow`, `/pay`, `/new`. Each POST returns 303 to `/` so refresh doesn't re-submit. State lives in a module-level dict keyed by a cookie session id (`hinc_sid`, 30-day expiry); restarting the server wipes in-progress runs — acceptable for now. Jinja2 template (`templates/game.html`) + CSS (`static/style.css`) mirror the terminal layout — dark bg, monospace, cyan accents, full provider price grid + open contracts table + action menu with `<details>` forms. End screen / bankruptcy screen render the same way the terminal renders them, just as HTML. Dispatch into the web frontend goes through `hallucination_inc.py --web` (still 15 lines, just with a flag check). Engine and terminal stay stdlib-only — Flask is the only runtime dep and goes in `requirements.txt`. CLAUDE.md updated to say "engine and terminal stdlib-only; other frontends may add deps." Smoke-tested end to end with curl: index renders in 10.6KB, POST /buy advances the day and updates inventory after a 303 → GET / round-trip. No web-level unit tests yet — added as a follow-up TODO.
- **2026-05-27** — **Engine / frontend split, step 3.** Split `test_hallucination_inc.py` (1314 lines) into `test_engine.py` (engine logic, ~670 lines, `import engine as g`) and `test_terminal.py` (UI + REPL, ~620 lines, `import terminal as g`), with the four shared fixtures (`_capture`, `_silent`, `_bare_state`, `_state_with`) lifted into `test_helpers.py`. The clean engine/UI boundary was already in the old file (line 718), so the split was mechanical — only the imports needed redirecting. With tests no longer routing through `hallucination_inc.py`, the transitional compat shim is gone: the launcher is now 15 lines (`from terminal import main; main()`). `run_tests.py` discovery still finds the new `test_*.py` files unchanged. 154 tests pass; coverage 92.7% engine / 96.0% terminal (unchanged from step 2). Updated CLAUDE.md layout block to reference the new test file structure.
- **2026-05-27** — **Engine / frontend split, step 2.** Moved the terminal UI into its own `terminal.py` module. `hallucination_inc.py` is now a thin entry-point launcher (`from terminal import main; main()`) plus a transitional compatibility shim that re-exports both the engine and terminal surfaces so `import hallucination_inc as g` still works for the existing test suite. Dropped a stray duplicate `compute_market_demand` that ended up in both engine and terminal during step 1. `run_tests.py` `TARGET_FILES` now `["engine.py", "terminal.py"]` — the launcher is pure import-time glue and not meaningful to coverage-gate. 154 tests still pass; coverage 92.7% engine / 96.0% terminal. Next: split tests along the new module boundary.
- **2026-05-27** — **Engine / frontend split, step 1.** Extracted all game logic into a new `engine.py` (constants, EVENTS, state, time, actions, oracles). `hallucination_inc.py` is now the terminal frontend: UI helpers, prompts, menus, end screens, REPL. It pulls the engine surface in via `from engine import *` plus an explicit list of `_`-prefixed helpers so existing tests that go through `import hallucination_inc as g` keep working unchanged. Added `is_bankrupt(state)` and `is_game_over(state)` as pure oracles on the engine so future frontends don't reimplement the end-condition logic. `simulate.py` now imports `engine` directly (it was already engine-only). `run_tests.py` tracks coverage on both files; 154 tests pass, coverage 91% engine / 95.5% terminal. CLAUDE.md updated — the "one file" rule is gone, replaced with an engine/frontend separation convention.
- **2026-05-27** — Added `test_hallucination_inc.py` (154 unittest tests) and `run_tests.py`, a stdlib-only coverage runner (uses `trace` + `ast` for line accounting — no pip deps). Threshold is 90%; current coverage of `hallucination_inc.py` is 95.6%. The runner is wired into `.git/hooks/pre-commit` (with a versioned copy at `scripts/pre-commit` and an installer at `scripts/install-hooks.sh` so fresh clones can run `./scripts/install-hooks.sh` to enable the gate). Hook only fires when staged files include `.py` or test files. Bypass with `--no-verify`.
- **2026-05-25** — Renamed the game from "Code Wars" / "PM Wars" / "Vibe Wars" to **Hallucination Inc.** in all UI copy and docs. Then renamed `pm_wars.py` → `hallucination_inc.py` and `docs/PM_Wars_PRD.md` → `docs/Hallucination_Inc_PRD.md`.
- **2026-05-25** — Surfaced in-progress build on the inventory dashboard's `Products:` line (alongside completed products) so remaining build days are visible without scanning the header.
- **2026-05-25** — Made the game properly turn-based: every successful action (buy/sell/craft/travel/borrow/pay) now advances exactly one day. Replaced the multi-day `Wait` prompt with a single-day `Next` action, moved to the end of the menu. `do_*` functions now return `(ok, msg)` tuples so menus can gate the day-advance on success; `do_travel` no longer advances internally. Simulator `execute()` mirrors the new rules. Planner win-rate dropped 66.5% → 46.0% (median NW $341k → $41k) as a result — flagged in TODO as the next rebalancing pass.
- **2026-05-26** — Big rebalance pass driven by 10K-run simulations. Five changes shipped:
  - **V1**: `DEBT_INTEREST` 5% → 3%/day, plus `+$75K` debt-free completion bonus at end screen.
  - **V2**: Compliance Dashboard nerfed (base $240K → $200K, recipe expanded). Brand Asset Generator (3d→2d, lighter recipe), Marketing Copilot (4d→3d, $90K→$95K), AI Customer Support (3d→2d, $70K→$85K) all buffed for faster-flip strategies.
  - **V3**: Active client roster expanded 4 → 6 via new `ACTIVE_CLIENT_COUNT` constant. Three mid-tier clients added (TechCrunch Startups, Local Gov Council, Etsy) with ≤0.55 min_quality so cheap providers (Google/Meta/Mistral) become viable.
  - **V4**: Final-craft variance tightened 0.92-1.05 → 0.96-1.04, `QUALITY_BONUS_CAP` 1.2 → 1.5.
  - **V5**: New starting-bundle choice in `main()` before `game_loop`. `new_game()` now takes optional `bundle`/`specialty_provider` params. Three options: YC Demo Day (+$50K cash / -5 days), Bootstrap Loan (+$50K cash / +$50K debt), Specialty Partnership (25% lifetime discount at chosen provider). `do_travel` re-applies the specialty discount on arrival so price refresh doesn't wipe it.
  - **Outcomes (10K planner runs)**: median NW $35K → $169K (4.9×), win rate 44% → 58%, soft-lock bankruptcy 5.9% → 4.0%, strategy gap planner-vs-random $197K → $261K. Google visits 224 → 2,282 (10×). Open issues: Compliance Dashboard still dominant at 55% of builds, Brand Asset Generator still rarely chosen by the bot, Meta still unvisited by the planner (logged in TODO).
- **2026-05-26** — Second-pass rebalance focused on killing the Compliance Dashboard monoculture. Three changes:
  - **Compliance Dashboard further nerfed**: base $200K → $150K, craft_days 5 → 6, recipe expanded to {Reasoning: 150, Code: 80, Image: 30}.
  - **V5 starting bundles removed** (per user request — wasn't pulling its weight in sim and added pre-game friction). `new_game()` back to no-arg; `_choose_starting_bundle`/`_choose_specialty_provider`/`_apply_specialty_discount` deleted; `do_travel` no longer re-applies discount.
  - **Low-tier clients reworked**: "TechCrunch Startups" → "Basecamp" (productivity-tools SMB). All three (Basecamp, Local Gov Council, Etsy) now have distinct specialties and wider budget_mult ranges (1.0-1.5 / 0.8-1.6 / 1.1-1.8) so deals vary more. Etsy leans into Brand Asset Generator with 1.1-1.8 mult to give that product a real customer.
  - **Outcomes**: Compliance Dashboard share 55.0% → 17.8% (monoculture broken). Top product now AI Security Scanner at 26.4%; five products at ≥10% share. Google visits 2.3K → 6.5K (3×), Mistral now occasionally visited (185). Trade-off: planner median NW $169K → $100K, win rate 58% → 50% — accepted cost for strategic diversity. Bankruptcy stayed at 3.7%. Brand Asset Generator still rare (0.3%) due to structural scarcity in want lists, not bot strategy.
- **2026-05-26** — Third-pass rebalance, closing the two open items from pass 2:
  - **Meta provider rescued**: quality 0.50 → 0.55 so it can clear mid-tier 0.55 contracts after the 0.96 variance floor. Planner now visits Meta 181 times per 10K games (was 0).
  - **Brand Asset Generator buffed**: base $55K → $65K. Added BAG to Walmart and Salesforce want lists so its demand pool grows from 4 → 6 client templates. Planner build share 0.3% → 1.0% (3.3×).
  - **simulate.py planner buffer corrected**: the bot's safe-quality buffer was `/0.92` (pre-V4 variance) in two spots; updated to `/0.96` to match the actual variance floor. Bot now sources marginally cheaper, which mildly favors recipe-heavy products like Compliance Dashboard.
  - **Outcomes (10K planner runs)**: BAG sales 230 (was 70), Meta visits 181 (was 0), product diversity 5 → 6 products at ≥10% share. Side effect: Compliance share crept 17.8% → 25.9% (bot's cheaper sourcing favored its large recipe); still well below the 55% monoculture. Median NW $100K → $77K, win rate 50% → 46.6% — small regression within noise, accepted for the diversity gains. New open item: Mistral now eclipsed by Meta (cheaper across the board, same effective quality tier).

- **2026-05-29** — Web frontend reskinned to the Claude Design "Glitch terminal" direction (locked Option 3 title palette → briefing + game-screen Option A "Terminal Classic"). Ported the handoff bundle's `game.css` tokens into a rewritten `src/static/style.css`; rebuilt `welcome.html` (briefing) and `game.html` (game) against the `.g-*` component vocabulary — chromatic `.g-glitch` wordmark, color-coded market quality + recipe chips (Co/Re/Im/Vo/Vi), GOV/ENT tags, restyled action dock + end screens. Added `_recipe_chips` view helper in `web.py`. Made it mobile-friendly with real CSS media queries (the design toggled `.g-mobile` from JS; a server-rendered page has one DOM, so `@media (max-width:640px)` reflows it and dense tables scroll horizontally). Engine + terminal untouched; Flask stays the only web dep. Verified both screens at desktop/mobile widths via the preview browser. One web smoke-test assertion updated (status bar now splits the day counter across label/value spans). 190 tests pass, web coverage 100%. See [ADR 005](../docs/adr/005-web-visual-design.md).

- **2026-05-29** — Added the animated boot-sequence title splash before the briefing (the locked "Glitch" direction from the design bundle). Vendored the framework-free `hallu-engine.js` into `src/static/`; `welcome.html` mounts it into a full-screen `#splash` overlay with the glitch config (boot log → ASCII column-sweep reveal of HALLUCINATION INC. → tagline + blinking "PRESS [ENTER] · TAP TO CONTINUE" + mock copyright; boot numbers aligned to $100K/$100K @ 3%/day). Enter/click/tap fades it out and reveals the briefing; scroll locked while up; `#splash` ships `hidden` so it degrades gracefully with JS off — existing `/start` flow and tests untouched. Tuned for the real (un-scaled) DOM: softened the engine's chromatic split from ±3px to ±2px (±1px mobile) so the dense 13-letter wordmark resolves, and sized it `clamp(7px,1.9vw,26px)` so the ~77-block-column line fits every viewport (925px @ 1280, 271px @ 375). Verified the full reveal + dismiss + briefing handoff on desktop and mobile via the preview browser. 190 tests pass. See [ADR 005](../docs/adr/005-web-visual-design.md).

- **2026-05-30** — Title splash made one-shot. The boot sequence was looping every ~11s (replaying boot → reveal endlessly), which read as "broken". Added a `cfg.loop` option to `hallu-engine.js` (`if (cfg.loop !== false) after(... runCycle)`) and pass `loop: false` from the splash config, so it plays once and settles on the logo. Kept the glitch flicker but slowed it from the engine's 3.4s to **30s** via `#splash ... .hi-word/.hi-inc { animation-duration: 30s }`. Verified: full reveal (545 chars), console hidden + footer shown, and stable past the old ~11s loop point (no re-cycle); computed animation-duration 30s on word + INC.

- **2026-05-30** — Reworked the splash glitch flicker from a fixed CSS loop to a JS-driven random schedule: first flicker 3s after the logo settles (gated on `.hi-footer.show`), then random 11–21s gaps (`setTimeout(flicker, 11000 + Math.random()*10000)`). CSS can't randomise intervals, so the base `.hi-word/.hi-inc` animation is now `none` and each flicker is one shot of a new `splashGlitch` keyframe (position jitter + intensified chromatic split) triggered by toggling `.glitch-now` on `#splash`. Respects `prefers-reduced-motion`. Timers cleared on dismiss. Verified base animation `none`, flickers fire + recur at 11–21s-consistent gaps, and a frozen peak frame reads as a deliberate glitch burst.

- **2026-05-30** — Added a **Quit** button to the web frontend's active-game screen. Posts to a new `POST /quit` route which ends the current run by forcing `state["day"] = engine.MAX_DAYS + 1`, so `engine.is_game_over()` flips true and the existing GAME OVER screen renders with the player's current cash/debt/products and locked-in final score — no new engine API needed. Button is bottom-right (`.g-quit` flex-end), reuses the existing `.g-btn.danger` red styling, and `onsubmit="return confirm(...)"` prompts before locking in. Context: a stale Flask process was still serving an older "Give up" variant of this button that had been removed from source (no git history of it); user asked to rename to Quit, which in practice meant re-adding the missing feature under the new name. Verified end-to-end via curl: button renders, POST /quit returns 303, follow-up GET shows the GAME OVER screen. 190 tests pass, web.py at 100% coverage.

- **2026-05-30** — Added ENTER keyboard support to the web frontend (two presentation-only changes). On the briefing (`welcome.html`), ENTER submits "Start the run" — handled in an IIFE outside the splash code so it works even with the JS splash engine absent, and guarded by `splashActive()` (checks `#splash` exists / not `hidden` / not `.dismissed`) so while the boot splash is up ENTER dismisses the splash instead of starting the game. On the game screen (`game.html`), ENTER submits the now-`id="next-day-form"` "Next day" form, but a `keydown` guard bails when `e.target.tagName` is INPUT/SELECT/TEXTAREA/BUTTON so ENTER still natively submits the Buy/Sell/Craft/Travel/Borrow/Pay forms when a field is focused. Both also guard `e.repeat`. Verified via preview browser: first ENTER on briefing dismisses splash (no start), second ENTER starts; on game screen ENTER-in-input is ignored while ENTER-on-body advances the day (1/30 → 2/30, "Day advanced."). No console errors; 194 tests pass, web coverage 100%.

- **2026-05-31** — Fixed a splash-dismissal bug reported on an Apple trackpad: the boot/title splash only listened for `click`, so a two-finger tap (which fires a *secondary* click — `mousedown/mouseup` button 2 + `contextmenu`/`auxclick`, but **no** `click` event) didn't dismiss it. The splash stayed up and looked like "Start did nothing / took me back to the start animation"; only a normal primary click got through (matching the user's "did it twice, third time worked"). Reproduced in the preview browser: a synthetic secondary `pointerdown`/`contextmenu` left `#splash` undismissed, a primary `click` dismissed it. Fix in `welcome.html`: dismiss on `pointerdown` (covers mouse any-button, touch, pen) and on `contextmenu` (with `preventDefault()` so the two-finger tap dismisses instead of opening the browser menu); kept `touchstart` + the `keydown` ENTER handling. Re-verified all three pointer paths dismiss and the menu is suppressed, the ENTER-to-start flow and the "Start the run" submit still reach `/start` and render the game, no console errors. 190 tests pass.

- **2026-06-19** — Stood up a real CI/CD pipeline, replacing the ungated single-stage Fly deploy (`fly-deploy.yml`, now removed) with `.github/workflows/ci.yml`: **test → deploy-staging → deploy-prod**. `test` runs on every push + PR (`pip install -r requirements.txt` — Flask is needed because `run_tests.py` imports `web.py` for coverage — then `python3 tests/run_tests.py`, Python pinned 3.12 to match the Dockerfile). `deploy-staging` (`needs: test`, main pushes only) deploys a new `hallucination-inc-staging` Fly app from `fly/fly.staging.toml` (scale-to-zero: `auto_stop_machines="stop"`, `min_machines_running=0` — safe because state is in-memory) then health-checks `https://hallucination-inc-staging.fly.dev/` (curl 200, 6×10s retry for cold starts). `deploy-prod` (`needs: deploy-staging`, main pushes only) deploys the existing prod app. Net: prod ships only if tests pass AND staging deploys AND staging answers healthy. Provisioned + first-deployed the staging app locally (`fly apps create` + `fly deploy --config fly/fly.staging.toml`); verified it serves 200 with the Briefing title. See [ADR 006](../docs/adr/006-ci-cd-pipeline.md). **Caveat:** both deploy jobs share the one `FLY_API_TOKEN` secret — it must be org-scoped (or cover both apps) or the staging job fails; an app-scoped prod-only deploy token won't work.

- **2026-06-20** — Reduced the active client board from 6 to 5 (`ACTIVE_CLIENT_COUNT` in `src/engine.py`). The constant is fully parameterized — both frontends' briefing copy ("N are live at once" in `welcome.html` / `terminal.py`) and the `test_engine.py` roster-size assertion derive from it, so no hardcoded "6" needed touching. Balance impact (per `tests/simulate.py`, unseeded so noisy; 3 baseline vs 4 post runs of 200 games): planner bankruptcy roughly flat (~6–11% → ~6–9.5%), win rate a touch lower (~33–37% → ~31–34%), and planner median net worth shifts from around break-even (~−$4K..+$6.5K) to clearly negative (~−$9K..−$47K). Fewer buyers = fewer selling opportunities, so it's a modest harder shift in the expected direction. Requested by the user. 190 tests pass.

- **2026-06-20** — Tightened the game screen's vertical spacing so the full board (header, inventory, market, open-contracts, action menu, Quit) fits one MacBook-14" screen (~880px viewport) without scrolling — no font shrink, nothing hidden. CSS-only in `src/static/style.css`: `.g-pad` 30/44→20/24 top/bottom padding, `.g-head` padding-bottom 16→12, `.g-sec` margin 26/12→15/8, `.g-inv` margin-top 18→12, `.g-banner` margin-top 16→10, `.g-table` th/td vertical padding 9→7, `.action-menu` margin/padding-top 28/20→18/12, `.g-quit` margin-top 24→14. Measured in the preview at 1512×880: content bottom dropped from 919px (39px overflow) to 824px at an 8-row contract board → ~56px headroom (covers heavier rosters too; max observed 8 rows with 5 clients). These classes are game-screen only, so the briefing (`.g-intro`/`.g-block`) is untouched; the mobile media query still overrides where it sets its own values, and the base trims make mobile marginally tighter as a bonus. 190 tests pass.

- **2026-06-21** — Set `ACTIVE_CLIENT_COUNT` back to 6 (was 5) per request — restores the original board size. Constant-driven, so briefing copy + roster test follow automatically. 190 tests pass. Note: 6 clients yields more open-contract rows (~10–12) than 5 (~6–8), so the game screen is taller again — the one-MacBook-14"-screen spacing tuning (2026-06-20) was sized around the 5-client board and may now overflow on a laptop viewport at busy rosters.

- **2026-06-21** — Realigned provider token prices + bumped Google quality to match the 2026 market (engine `PROVIDERS`). Fixed the premium-ordering inversion so price tracks quality: Anthropic Reasoning 30→50 (now the price leader, ≈ real Fable 5 $50/M output), OpenAI Reasoning 50→30 (≈ real GPT-5.5 $30/M). Google quality 0.70→0.80 (Gemini 3 is frontier-class in 2026). Balance impact (`tests/simulate.py`, unseeded/noisy; 2 before vs 3 after runs of 200 planner games): harder shift — win rate ~33–43% → ~22–30%, bankruptcy ~6% → ~8–9%, planner median net worth ~break-even → clearly negative (~−$35K..−$71K). Cause: the inversion fix removes the old "buy top-quality Anthropic reasoning cheaply ($30, q0.95)" sweet spot — premium reasoning is the biggest recipe consumer (Compliance Dashboard 150 Re, Contract Analyzer / AI Security Scanner 80 Re), so margins compress; the Google 0.80 buff softens but doesn't offset because high-payout contracts need ≥85% quality Google can't hit alone. Tested a softer variant (Anthropic Reasoning 40) — recovers only a few points, ~27–33% win. Shipped the realistic frontier values per user request (realism over balance); softening lever (Reasoning 50→40) or a compensating recipe/value buff left open in TODO. 190 tests pass.

- **2026-06-21** — Capped the open-contracts board to the top 7 by payout (new engine constant `MAX_VISIBLE_CONTRACTS = 7`). The board lists one row per (active client × wanted product), which ran ~10–12 rows with 6 clients; both frontends now slice to the top 7 after sorting — `web._contracts_view` returns `rows[:MAX_VISIBLE_CONTRACTS]`, `terminal.show_open_contracts` slices likewise. Pure presentation cap: it doesn't change `active_clients`, daily rotation, or what you can sell when you travel to a client (lower-payout contracts still exist, just aren't surfaced on the board). Side benefit: with 6 clients the game screen had grown past one MacBook-14" viewport again; capping to 7 rows brings it back within one screen (verified 7/7 rows across 8 fresh rosters + a 1512×880 screenshot — full board, market, action menu, Quit all above the fold). The web glance summary now reads "≤7 live". 190 tests pass, web.py still 100%.

- **2026-07-01** — Built the **Linear board → auto-build/ship loop**: move a card to **Todo** and an in-session Claude builds it; move a card **out of QA** and Claude ships it to prod. Poll-based (Linear can't push to a laptop), but since moving a card *is* the trigger it fires within one interval. Three pieces: `scripts/linear_watch.py` (stdlib-only pure diff engine — reads the board + a git-ignored local snapshot `.claude/linear_watch_state.json`, returns `to_build` = cards in Todo and `to_ship` = cards that were in QA last tick and have since moved out; no git/Fly/build side effects, can self-fetch with `LINEAR_API_KEY` for CLI use but the loop path feeds it the board via the authenticated Linear MCP so no key needed), `.claude/commands/linear-tick.md` (the `/linear-tick` slash command = one poll + act: Todo → In Progress → branch → build → `run_tests.py` → commit locally → `fly deploy --config fly/fly.staging.toml` → QA + proof comment, **never** touching `main`; then on QA-exit → merge to `main` + push → CI test→staging→prod → Done + proof), and `docs/linear-autobuild.md` (the how/why + one-time setup). Driven by the built-in `/loop 3m /linear-tick` in an open session. The staging-vs-prod split is deliberate: CI (ADR 006) ships prod on every `main` push, so the build phase stays on a branch + local staging deploy and prod ships **only** when you approve by leaving QA. Also taught `sync_todo_from_linear.py` a new **QA** section (between In Progress and In Review) so the generated TODO.md stays correct. **One-time manual step:** add a `QA` workflow state (Started group) in the Mics-playground team — the Linear MCP has no create-status call and no key is exported here, so it can't be automated. Diff engine verified across first-run / QA-exit / quiet transitions; 190 tests still pass, coverage unchanged.

- **2026-06-21** — Made **Linear the source of truth for the backlog**; `docs/TODO.md` is now a generated, read-only mirror (ADR 007). Added `scripts/sync_todo_from_linear.py` — stdlib-only (`urllib`+`json`, matching the zero-install convention), one-way pull (Linear GraphQL → file), reads `LINEAR_API_KEY` from env. Maps issue priority → section (Urgent/High→Now, Medium→Next, Low/None→Later), state type → checkbox (`completed`→[x], `started`→[~], else [ ]; canceled dropped, completed→Done); every line links back to its `MIC-…` issue. Output is deterministic and timestamp-free, so it's idempotent; `--check` exits non-zero on drift (CI/pre-commit ready, not yet wired — needs the key as a secret). Seeded the Linear `pm_game` project earlier with 15 issues (MIC-5..19) from the old TODO; marked MIC-6/MIC-7 In Progress to match reality. Regenerated `docs/TODO.md` from live Linear state and verified `render()` reproduces it byte-for-byte via a mock-issue unit check. `CLAUDE.md` "Memory & docs maintenance" updated: edit the backlog in Linear, never hand-edit `docs/TODO.md`. The previous rich inline TODO notes (e.g. 2026 token-price research) now live in issue descriptions + this file.

- **2026-06-21** — Added `scripts/setup_linear_board.py` (one-off, idempotent, stdlib + `LINEAR_API_KEY`) to configure the Linear board columns the MCP can't touch (workflow states are read-only via the integration). It renames the stock `Backlog`→`later` and `Todo`→`now`, creates a `next` state, orders them `later → next → now → In Progress → In Review → Done`, and moves the pm_game issues into now/next/later by priority (Urgent/High→now, Medium→next, Low/None→later; started/done issues left alone). Direct Linear GraphQL (`workflowStateCreate/Update`, `issueUpdate`) since the MCP exposes no state-mutation tools. User runs it once with their key. Follow-up once it's run: switch `sync_todo_from_linear.py` from priority-based sections to state-based (now/next/later/In Progress/In Review/Done) so `docs/TODO.md` mirrors the board model.

- **2026-06-21** — Switched `sync_todo_from_linear.py` from priority-based to **state-based** grouping so `docs/TODO.md` mirrors the Linear board columns: sections are now Later / Next / Now / In Progress / In Review / Done (was Now/Next/Later by priority). Maps workflow-state name → section (later|backlog→Later, next→Next, now→Now, "in progress"→In Progress, "in review"→In Review, done→Done; unknown names fall back by type), checkbox by state type. Regenerated `docs/TODO.md` from the live state pulled via the Linear MCP — the board setup hasn't been run yet, so all 13 backlog issues currently sit under **Later** and MIC-6/MIC-7 under **In Progress**; running `scripts/setup_linear_board.py` (creates now/next/later columns, moves issues by priority) + re-sync will redistribute High→Now, Medium→Next, Low→Later. Verified render() reproduces the file byte-for-byte against the live data. ADR 007 mapping updated. 190 tests pass.

- **2026-06-21** — Removed `scripts/setup_linear_board.py`. It only existed to create the now/next/later board columns via raw Linear GraphQL (the MCP can't mutate workflow states), but the board is staying on the default columns, so the script had no remaining purpose. Everything else (issue moves, regenerating `docs/TODO.md`) is done in-session through the connected Linear integration — no standalone script/key needed for that. Updated the sync-script docstring and ADR 007 to drop the references; workflow states are managed in Linear's team settings if ever wanted. `sync_todo_from_linear.py` (the CI-checked file mirror) is unaffected.

- **2026-07-01** — Finished **MIC-7** (browser action hotkeys). Extended the web frontend's keyboard handling in `src/templates/game.html` from ENTER-only to a full single-key scheme: **B**uy / **S**ell / **C**raft / **T**ravel / B**o**rrow / **P**ay open the matching action panel, **Enter** advances the day, **Esc** closes the open panel (or bails out of a focused field). Implementation is pure presentation — the panels are already CSS-driven by hidden radio inputs, so a hotkey just sets the corresponding radio `checked` (CSS reveals the panel) and moves focus to the panel's first `input`/`select` for immediate typing. Same typing-guard as the existing ENTER handler (ignores keys while target is INPUT/SELECT/TEXTAREA/BUTTON, and skips when Ctrl/Meta/Alt held) so typing a quantity like "10b" never trips a shortcut; Buy/Sell radios only exist at the relevant location, so their keys are no-ops elsewhere. Discoverability: each action label underlines its trigger letter (`<u>`) + has a `title="Shortcut: X"`; Next-day button now reads "Next day ↵". Verified in the browser preview (preview MCP): all six panels open + focus correctly, Esc closes, typing-guard holds, `s` is a no-op at a provider and `b` a no-op at a client, and Sell opens after travelling to a client. 190 tests pass, web.py coverage still 100% (no Python changed). Marked MIC-7 **Done** in Linear and regenerated `docs/TODO.md` — note this also corrected pre-existing drift: MIC-6 had reverted to Backlog in Linear but the stale file still listed it (and MIC-7) under In Progress, so the regen moved MIC-6 to Later.

- **2026-07-01** — Extended the Linear workflow mechanism (CLAUDE.md "Memory & docs maintenance"): when a ticket is finished, always close the loop on its Linear issue — move it to the right state and add (1) links to every git commit (full GitHub commit URL + a Linear link attachment), (2) proof of work (test results + coverage; browser/preview verification with a screenshot for visual/web changes), and (3) a short 1–3 sentence description of what was done. Tools: `save_comment` for the write-up, `save_issue` `links` for the commit attachment. Applied retroactively to MIC-7 (commit 42ae6a0 attached + a Done comment with the shortcut list, 190-test/coverage results, and a per-key browser-verification matrix).
